title: TanStack Start docs
claims: RC full-stack framework powered by TanStack Router; SSR, streaming, server functions/RPCs; deploy anywhere JS runs; client-side-first DX.
caveats: RC/newer maturity risk.

title: SvelteKit introduction
claims: Robust performant web apps; routing, SSR/CSR/prerendering, image optimization, Vite HMR.
caveats: Requires buying into Svelte ecosystem.

title: State of JS 2025 Meta-frameworks
claims: Next.js power comes with learning-curve/DX downsides; SvelteKit has strong satisfaction signal.
caveats: Survey sample bias; popularity not decision criterion.

title: Hono docs
claims: Small, simple, ultrafast web-standard framework; works on Workers, Deno, Bun, Lambda, Vercel, Netlify, Node.
caveats: Minimal core means choosing separate validation/openapi patterns.

title: Fastify docs
claims: Mature Node web framework option for long-running services.
caveats: Less edge-native than Hono.

title: Drizzle ORM site
claims: TypeScript ORM with active releases, migrations, RLS support, Neon/Supabase imports.
caveats: Still below 1.0 in parts; migration edge cases exist.

title: Prisma docs
claims: High-DX ORM with mature ecosystem.
caveats: More abstraction and runtime/client weight than Drizzle.

title: Better Auth
claims: Modern TypeScript authentication candidate.
caveats: You own more auth/security responsibility than with hosted IdP.

title: Clerk pricing/docs
claims: Hosted auth accelerates teams but MAU/add-on pricing and lock-in matter.
caveats: Cost can grow with users/features.

title: Inngest documentation
claims: Event-driven durable execution; TypeScript/Python/Go; handles queues, state, scaling, throttling, observability.
caveats: Managed service lock-in; self-hosting posture must be checked per workload.

title: Trigger.dev docs
claims: Open-source background jobs/workflows in async code; retries, monitoring, realtime API; cloud or self-host.
caveats: More worker/container oriented; evaluate cloud limits and self-host ops.

title: Cloudflare Workers pricing
claims: Paid Workers minimum $5/month; 10M requests + 30M CPU ms included; no egress charge for Workers; examples show low dynamic costs.
caveats: CPU/runtime/API limits; platform lock-in across KV/Durable Objects/R2/Queues.

title: Railway pricing
claims: Usage-based compute; Hobby $5 minimum credits, Pro $20; replicas, logs, cron, private networking, databases/storage.
caveats: Egress charged; observability/log retention plan-bound.

title: Fly.io resource pricing
claims: VM machine pricing by CPU/RAM; global regions; volumes and Postgres billed separately.
caveats: Operationally closer to infra than PaaS; storage cleanup can surprise bills.

title: Neon pricing
claims: Serverless Postgres; pay-as-you-go; scale-to-zero; branching; Launch compute $0.106/CU-hour, storage $0.35/GB-month.
caveats: Cold resume/connection behavior needs load testing; monitoring retention limited by plan.

title: Supabase pricing
claims: Postgres-backed app platform with auth/storage/realtime/edge functions.
caveats: Convenience can couple app to platform-specific services.

title: Upstash pricing
claims: Serverless Redis/Vector/QStash candidate for low-ops cache/queue primitives.
caveats: Per-request/serverless model not ideal for hot high-throughput workloads.

title: OpenTelemetry JavaScript docs
claims: JS traces and metrics stable; logs development; browser instrumentation experimental.
caveats: Use Sentry/PostHog/vendor SDKs for product-level UX until browser OTel matures.

title: Sentry pricing
claims: Developer free; Team $26/mo; Business $80/mo at access date.
caveats: Event volumes/session replay can drive cost.

title: PostHog pricing
claims: Generous free tiers: 1M analytics events, 5K replay recordings, 1M feature flag requests, 100K exceptions.
caveats: Analytics volume and retention drive spend.

title: shadcn/ui docs
claims: Not a component library; distributes editable component code; composition and AI-ready/open-code model.
caveats: You own maintenance of copied components.

title: Tailwind CSS Vite installation
claims: Zero-runtime CSS generated from scanned source; Vite plugin is seamless for SvelteKit/React Router/Nuxt/Solid.
caveats: Utility-first style may not suit all teams.

title: Stripe documentation
claims: Broad docs for payments, subscriptions, usage-based billing, customer portal, Checkout/Elements.
caveats: Complex revenue models still require careful webhook/accounting design.

title: Resend pricing
claims: Free 3,000 transactional emails/month; Pro $20 for 50k; React Email, SMTP/API/webhooks included.
caveats: Marketing email contacts priced separately.

title: Meilisearch overview
claims: Single-binary REST API search; typo tolerant, semantic/RAG claims, under-50ms claim from vendor.
caveats: Vendor performance claim needs workload-specific verification.
